source.title: How to Set Up and Defend Outback - Every Reinforcement, Rotate, and Head Hole (all four sites)
source.notes: Auto-generated captions (creator referred to as 'Litton' in the intro). Covers all four Outback sites: Piano/Laundry, Party/Office (Bull), Green/Red Bedroom, and Mechanic/Kitchen. No attack-side transcript is available for this map. Room/callout names are the creator's own informal terms (he explicitly says Outback callouts 'need some work' and invents names like 'coffee door'/'vending door' on the fly) — not verified against wiki callouts. A few operator names are garbled by ASR; see notes on individual entries where confidence is not high. Compass directions could not be reliably grounded for this map (Ubisoft's official blueprint asset for Outback returns 404 on their CDN, and no labeled top-down callout diagram with confirmed orientation was found) — locations are left as room/feature names only, no compass prefixes, per spec rule 8.

reinforcements.location: Two Right-Hand Walls of Piano into Games Room
reinforcements.note: First two of seven reinforcements on this site.

reinforcements.location: Right-Hand Wall of Showers into Games Room
Right-Hand Wall of Laundry into Top End of Showers
reinforcements.note: One of the two main breach walls.

reinforcements.location: Left-Hand Wall of Laundry into Laundry Corridor
reinforcements.note: Final reinforcement of the seven used on this site.
Not reinforced in this setup, but do it if you have spare reinforcements. Stops attackers sledge/book-holing across from Bull once they control it — forces them through one door instead.

headHoles.location: Wall Across from Games into Barbecue
headHoles.note: Lets a defender in Games hold onto the door and this angle through to Barbecue.
Lets you fall back from Games to Showers and hold from a different angle; also lets you hold the Games door from a tight angle.
Stand on the washing machine, make one hole. Holds the breach and the Shower window on a tight angle. Easy to fall off — don't overcommit.
More useful late-round than early — the nearby window is busy mid-round.
Use when attackers push Games without pressuring Barbecue — push around this side and use these angles.

rotates.note: Line it up roughly in the middle of the wall — hidden from the door, still behind the box/bomb crouched. Too far either way and the door sees you.
Check the nearby window barricade is up first — unusable if it's down and held. Use late-round to escape past someone holding the main door, or to escape Kids/get out of Laundry.

hatches.note: Keep open. Drop it and come back up if you end up in a bad spot in Piano.

gadgetPlacements.note: Kiba Barrier here hides the Games defender from the window while holding this angle through to Barbecue. Source audio said 'a zomi'/'keyo barrier' — high-confidence read given the gadget name match.
Shoot the flower pot once (don't punch it) so it survives, then use it plus the banister to stand and hold the bottom of the stairs from an unexpected angle.

Place an info/trap gadget here. Source audio said 'a prox mine, an Al mine, a banshe' — 'banshe' likely matches Melusi's Banshee Sensor, but 'Al mine' is unclear and unconfirmed. Always have some form of early warning on these stairs regardless of operator.
Use the reinforcement here for cover; peek the breach and watch the window from this spot.
Hold from Showers, not Dorms itself. Watch the window and the walk-in from the breach.
Shield covers the window; move around behind the shield to also cover the breach angle. No operator confirmed for the shield.

operatorRecommendations.note: Holds Games with a Kiba Barrier on the window angle. Hold as long as possible without dying, fall back once pressured through the window or from Bull.

operatorRecommendations.operator: Unconfirmed — source audio said 'ideally as Army', no real Defense operator match
operatorRecommendations.note: Do not guess a specific operator; treat as unconfirmed until verified against the video.

generalTips.note: Hold Games without dying — kill a few drones, eat some flashbangs, get a kill if you can, don't overstay. Losing Games within 20 seconds gives attackers control of Showers, Laundry, and Piano.
The Shower window breach is the one most attackers open — know your safe standing spots relative to it.
Shower window peek: line up the angle before crouching, wait for the pixel/movement on the side of the window, pre-fire once you hear them.
Weaker site than Party/Office — prefer defending Party/Office if you have the choice.
Needs a decent roamer — someone anchoring Games to start, plus someone roaming Bull/Office/Party/top of Bull Stairs.

reinforcements.note: Always reinforce — leaving soft lets attackers sneak under the rafters and come up at the top of Garage Stairs unopposed.

reinforcements.location: Right-Hand Wall of Office into Garage (Supply)
reinforcements.note: Third and fourth of ten reinforcements on this site.

reinforcements.location: Middle Two Walls of Party into Bull
Right Wall of Party-Office Quad Wall
reinforcements.note: Reinforce only this one of the four wall segments — keeps attackers guessing which side to worry about.

reinforcements.location: Two Walls from Bull into Games
reinforcements.note: Final two reinforcements, ten total on this site.

headHoles.location: Behind the Bomb, Angled onto Door
headHoles.note: Angle onto the door from behind the bomb.

headHoles.location: Two Low Head Holes onto Shark Stairs
headHoles.note: Keep them low and small — cheeky angle onto the top of Shark/Bull Stairs without giving attackers a good angle back.
Destroy the papers/boxes near the breach, tuck in and hole only the breach wall (not the shelf) for a hidden angle on the breach.
Holds Garage Stairs for anyone coming up.

rotates.note: Needed — without it the only way from Party back to Office is the long way via Electrical and Supply.

rotates.location: Two Side Walls of Party-Office Quad Wall
rotates.note: Leave both soft. Lets defenders peek the door or window from this side, forcing attackers exiting Bull to worry about multiple angles instead of droning one reinforced wall.
Lets a defender pop out and fight into Garage late-round. Keep it low enough to peek but not vault through — the nearby box allows vaulting.

gadgetPlacements.note: Mute this hole — gives intel denial for the whole site.
Mute here — most attacks come through this approach if not the main breach.
Place on either side, right side preferred so it isn't immediately visible to attackers coming up Garage Stairs.
Can't be smoked from Garage door; line of sight to hold Garage door; use the reinforcement as cover.

gadgetPlacements.location: Prone Punch Hole, Lined with Office Door
gadgetPlacements.note: Small punch hole at floor level, prone, in line with the door. Attackers focus on the big holes above and miss it — free kill on rushers. Don't camp it the whole round.
Get up on a raised position — your head sits at a height attackers peeking Bull don't expect, sometimes blocked by their own sight housing.

operatorRecommendations.holdLocation: Drone Hole / Games Door / Garage Stairs Door
operatorRecommendations.note: Runs three jammer placements: the drone hole, the Games-door approach into Bull, and the door near Garage Stairs.

generalTips.note: Ten reinforcements used in total — brief the team, it's more involved to set up than Games/Laundry.
Head holes onto Shark Stairs let attackers cut off part of the site by holding that angle — counter by starting a player in Games/Laundry/Showers to contest the far end and force drones to be spent.
Destroying an attacker drone near this end of the map then repositioning tells attackers someone's there without revealing where — useful misdirection.
More solid overall than Piano/Laundry despite needing more setup and coordination.

reinforcements.location: Two Walls from Closet into Coffee Door
reinforcements.note: Creator improvises this callout on the spot ('coffee door'/'vending door') — not a verified wiki callout.

reinforcements.location: Two Walls from Red Bedroom into Bottom of Yellow Stairs
Two Walls from Red Bedroom into Reptile Hallway
reinforcements.note: Reinforcements 5 and 6. Consider leaving one soft and making a rotate there instead — see rotates below.
Final two reinforcements, same style as the Piano/Laundry upstairs area — needed to hold the footprint above Green and Red Bedroom.

headHoles.note: Holds the bathroom door from the upstairs footprint.
Same technique on the other bathroom door.

The single most important vertical on this site — most attacking teams open the wall breach, smoke it off, and plant tucked in this corner where a downstairs defender has almost no way to contest. Hold this hole from above.

headHoles.location: Vertical Above Reptile Hallway/Bottom Yellow Door
headHoles.note: Holds people coming from Reptile Hallway and from the bottom Yellow door, from above. Watch your back for anyone coming through the bridge.
Holds down onto that door from above.
Move the bolt on the wardrobe and hole through it to hold the door.

rotates.location: Two Rotates at Green/Red Bedroom Dividing Wall
rotates.note: Lets defenders move between the two halves of the site; pair with a line of sight across the middle. Green Bedroom is generally the safer side — pushes usually come from Red.
Crouch through — generally safe hiding behind the flight cases on the other side.

rotates.location: Vault Rotate onto Reptile Hallway (via cabinet)
rotates.note: Destroy the cabinet with impact charge or C4 (shotgun won't work), hole it, climb up and vault over. Useful if trapped in Closet, or to pressure Reptile Hallway.

rotates.location: Optional Rotate at Red Bedroom/Reptile Hallway Wall
rotates.note: Alternative to fully reinforcing this wall — lets a pressured defender appear behind a tank prop to pressure Reptile Hallway. Only make this if you'll actually play around that area afterward.
Lets a defender hold that angle for free from Reptile, at the cost of the wall being soft there.

gadgetPlacements.note: Barricades this door, this door, this window, and that window, then turtles in upstairs with a teammate.
Leave open (not barricaded) as an escape route back down to site.

Early warning gadget for anyone launching through the bathroom window. Source audio said 'a prox m or Maly Banshee' — likely Melusi's Banshee Sensor, same read as the Piano/Laundry stairs reference, but not fully confirmed.

operatorRecommendations.note: Barricades all four upstairs entry points and turtles in with a partner, holding the vertical above both bedrooms.

generalTips.note: Holding upstairs is critical — the footprint sits directly above Green Bedroom, Red Bedroom, and roughly Piano too. Hold it hard but don't die up there.
Any rotate or line of sight you make needs a defender actually playing near it — an abandoned rotate just hands attackers a free path.
The breach corner is an attacker's dream if left uncontested from above — they only need to breach and plant, with downstairs largely unable to stop them.
Attackers often rush in expecting to plant uncontested — the upstairs vertical holes usually get you a free kill.

reinforcements.location: Two Walls from Mechanic into Waiting
reinforcements.note: First two of eight reinforcements (four downstairs, four upstairs). Creator calls the room 'Lorry' informally; official name is Waiting.

reinforcements.location: Two Reinforcements on Lower Garage Wall (going upstairs)

reinforcements.note: Fifth and sixth reinforcements, mirrors the Party/Office setup.

reinforcements.location: Two Reinforcements on Middle Party Walls
reinforcements.note: Final two reinforcements — leaves the outer walls soft to force attackers to worry about multiple angles.

headHoles.location: Upstairs Line of Sight onto Garage Stairs
headHoles.note: Mirrors the Garage Stairs angle from the Party/Office site.

headHoles.location: Upstairs Line of Sight onto Shark Stairs

headHoles.note: Not reinforced in this run, but worth doing.

rotates.note: Paired with a second rotate and a line of sight across, same pattern as other sites' upstairs setups.
Paired with the first upstairs rotate.
One on each side — lets you get through both sides of the wall.

Don't reinforce this wall — make a rotate instead. Kitchen has two doors and is hard to play directly; this lets you escape, or aggress onto Garage, without using the two sight rotates that might be watched from Garage.

rotates.location: Rotate toward Reptile Hallway / Lobby Door
rotates.note: Needs a team that knows what it's doing. Lets a defender hold the Lobby door from a different angle, aggress onto it or the other door, then rotate back unpredictably.

hatches.note: Site is soft above on both plant locations. Open this hatch to drop back into site after holding upstairs.

gadgetPlacements.note: Natural cover; good ACOG-style loadout spot to hold angles onto the door and around onto Garage. No operator named for this position.

generalTips.note: Under-explored site — has potential but rarely played.
Hold upstairs hard — attackers opening the vertical above badly restricts defender movement — but fall back through the hatch before you die.
Holding the Mechanic door, the window, and down onto Reception are key downstairs responsibilities.
Needs more team coordination and pre-round planning than Party/Office.
If solo queuing without coordinated teammates, skip the deeper rotate plays (e.g. Reptile Hallway/Lobby rotate).
